methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $350M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Fairmat, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Benjamin Saada, has raised approximately €95M ($103M) to date against a current post-money valuation of $350M. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same-stage private companies spanning $300M to $10.0B, supplemented by one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier against the $350M base, implies $665M. Peer-median valuation across the five robotics comparables—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—yields $1.6B. The wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) reflects material differentiation in market positioning and traction across the robotics subsector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $565M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.2B. Current valuation sits at the low end of this range. Key uncertainties include differentiation relative to higher-valued peers, revenue visibility, and near-term capital requirements. The peer set composition—dominated by private valuations with limited public market reference—creates dependency on private round pricing efficiency and exit precedent within robotics manufacturing.
